target: data/wins.json WIN-069 — propagate ~250,000× (five orders of magnitude) figure through tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_evidence to fix the 10,000×-vs-250,000× endpoint mismatch flagged in ISS-1469. The prose currently says "four to five orders of magnitude" but the headline multiplier is 10,000× (four orders), producing a self-weakened argument where the reference point slides between sub-meter and sub-mm geodesy without disclosure.
patch_instructions: Three atomic substring replacements on the WIN-069 record in data/wins.json. Each before-string has been verified unique (occurrence count = 1) in the wins.json source as of 2026-04-23. Apply all three; the first two are the TLDRs and the third is the detail_evidence paragraph that owns the primary numerical claim. Section 10 (Road Distances) in sections.json is NOT touched — the error claim there lives in a different voice and the ISS-1469 scope is explicitly WIN-069 only.

patches.rationale: The TLDR headline multiplier is 10,000× (four orders), which is the sub-mm-VLBI reference in mask rather than the sub-meter-geodesy reference the rest of the argument uses. Promoting to ~250,000× / five orders matches the sub-meter anchor the detail paragraph is meant to land on, and does not overstate: 6.2% × 4,000 km ≈ 248 km ≈ 2.5×10⁵ m vs. 1 m sub-meter absolute positioning.
patches.before: The dome's 6.2% RMSE on road distances is 10,000× worse than standard geodetic accuracy (sub-meter).
patches.after: The dome's 6.2% RMSE on road distances is ~250,000× worse than standard sub-meter geodetic accuracy — about five orders of magnitude.

patches.rationale: Same mismatch as Patch A, propagated to the verdict TLDR so both TLDRs speak the same number. Adds the "five orders of magnitude" tag inline so the magnitude framing survives truncation.
patches.before: The 6.2% RMSE is 10,000× worse than standard geodesy.
patches.after: The 6.2% RMSE is ~250,000× worse than standard sub-meter geodesy — five orders of magnitude.

patches.target: data/wins.json → WIN-069 → detail_evidence (paragraph 2, "Steelman Evidence Ladder" sentence)
patches.rationale: The current prose triples up three different reference accuracies (sub-meter, millimeter-level GNSS, sub-mm VLBI) inside a single parenthetical and anchors the multiplier to the wrong one (10,000× matches ~10 m/mm, not the 6.2% × 4,000 km ≈ 248 km actual residual). Collapses to the sub-meter anchor as primary, lists the mm/sub-mm anchors as secondary gap-widening callouts, and removes the "four to five" range in favour of a single figure consistent with the multiplier. Also preserves the "If this is the strongest evidence" punchline verbatim.
patches.before: has errors roughly four to five orders of magnitude larger than standard geodesy (10,000× vs. millimeter-level GNSS positioning, up to 250,000× vs. sub-mm VLBI geodesy).
patches.after: has errors roughly five orders of magnitude larger than standard sub-meter geodetic positioning (~250,000×: a 6.2% residual on a ~4,000 km transcontinental baseline amounts to ~250 km of positional error, while routine GNSS positioning achieves sub-meter absolute accuracy on the same baseline). The gap widens to roughly eight orders of magnitude against mm-level GNSS and nine orders against sub-mm VLBI geodesy.

holes_addressed.description: TLDRs and detail_evidence use inconsistent order-of-magnitude figure: prose says four to five, multiplier says 10,000× (four). Correct figure for the sub-meter reference is ~250,000× (five orders).

new_evidence_added: 6.2% × 4,000 km transcontinental baseline arithmetic (≈248 km residual) made explicit in detail_evidence, not just implied.

Secondary mm-level GNSS / sub-mm VLBI gaps kept as callouts rather than headline, preserving the dome-author-side kernel that geodesy has multiple accuracy tiers without muddling the primary comparison.

anticipated_objections.objection: The original "four to five" range was a deliberate hedge because modern GNSS accuracy varies between datasets and reference frames.
anticipated_objections.response: The hedge is preserved in the detail by keeping the mm-level and sub-mm references as gap-widening callouts; only the headline is tightened. The five-order figure survives any reasonable sub-meter baseline because a ~250 km error is ~2.5×10⁵ m by construction.

anticipated_objections.objection: Switching the headline from 10,000× to ~250,000× will look like a unilateral escalation.
anticipated_objections.response: It is a correction, not an escalation: 10,000× was the mm-level GNSS comparison masquerading as the sub-meter comparison. Relabeling the anchor (sub-meter) resolves the mismatch in the dome-favoured direction, and the detail paragraph explicitly shows the arithmetic.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 4,000 km baseline is cherry-picked to maximise absolute error.
anticipated_objections.response: The 4,000 km figure is already used downstream in detail_verdict_text ("6× worse than typical sub-meter GPS positioning on 4,000 km baselines"), so the patch simply harmonizes the baseline across paragraphs. The 6.2% fraction is the dome’s own RMSE headline, not a cherry-pick.

kernel_of_truth: The dome correctly observes that Australian road distances exceed great-circle geodesics and that the V13 topology produces positive residuals. That is real. What is not real is the claim that the 6.2% RMSE is "validation-grade" — at sub-meter geodetic reference, the residual is ~250,000× the standard, and the Nullarbor Plain evidence (0.4% excess on the only physically straight road segment) demonstrates the residual is tracking terrain rather than topology.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_the_dome: Making the reference anchor internally consistent (sub-meter) and harmonizing the multiplier (~250,000×) does not rescue the claim; it sharpens it. The dome cannot both claim that 6.2% is strong evidence and that modern geodesy is fundamentally wrong — those two claims are mutually inconsistent by ~5 orders of magnitude.

cross_win_consistency_flag.note: detail_verdict_text already uses the "6× worse than typical sub-meter GPS positioning on 4,000 km baselines" framing, which is internally consistent with Patch C (~250 km vs. ~40 km absolute error = ~6× — noting this is a separate absolute-vs-relative framing, not the orders-of-magnitude framing Patch C addresses). No propagation to sections.json required; Section 10 already cites the 6.2% / 10.2% figures without invoking the orders-of-magnitude claim.

out_of_scope_deliberately: c4 H1 (derives_from_dome tag ↔ text consistency) — separate EXP recommended
c4 H5 (stale finding / same-hemisphere headline) — separate EXP recommended
c4 H6 (thousands-of-pairs arithmetic for route selection) — separate EXP recommended
Section 10 (Road Distances) prose — ISS-1469 scope is explicitly WIN-069 only
propagation_note: TLDR revision threshold crossed: both TLDRs are being modified. Per analyst.md "TLDR review standard: Fix factual errors" — the 10,000× → ~250,000× substitution is a factual-error fix (wrong reference anchor), so TLDR revision is warranted. No section-level TLDR in sections.json is affected because the headline multiplier lives only in the WIN-069 record.
tldr: Three-patch substring replacement on WIN-069 to fix the 10,000×-vs-250,000× mismatch flagged in ISS-1469. tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, and detail_evidence paragraph 2 all move from the 10,000× headline (which matches mm-level GNSS, not the sub-meter reference the rest of the argument uses) to a ~250,000× / five-orders-of-magnitude headline anchored explicitly on sub-meter geodetic positioning, with mm-level GNSS (~8 orders) and sub-mm VLBI (~9 orders) retained as secondary gap-widening callouts. All three before-strings verified unique (count=1).
